title: Three reasons why a food-supply shock may be coming to Southeast Asia: Goldman Sachs
summary_full: Higher input costs and a potential El Niño weather event are combining to increase the risk of significant food price inflation across Southeast Asia, according to a report from Goldman Sachs
summary_public: Higher input costs and a potential El Niño weather event are combining to increase the risk of significant food price inflation across Southeast Asia, according to a report from Goldman Sachs
